Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 071 [Prayer to Nabu][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-na ⸢er-ši rap-ša₂ uz-ni IGI.GAL₂⸣ ša₂-⸢ma-mi⸣

(o 1) To the wise one with extensive intelligence, the wise man of the heavens, designer of the plans of the Apsu, establisher of the inhabited world.

o 22

mu-uṣ-ṣir ⸢u₂⸣-<ṣu>-rat ⸢ABZU⸣ mu-⸢kin₂ da-ad₂-me⸣

o 33

a-na d⸢NA₃ er⸣-ši rap-ša₂ ⸢uz⸣-ni IGI.GAL₂ ⸢ša₂-ma-mi⸣

(o 3) To Nabu, the wise one with extensive intelligence, the wise man of the heavens, designer of the plans of the Apsu, establisher of the inhabited world, giver of strewn offerings to [...], burner of incense offerings, Šamaš by day, Sin by night, the brightest, the most radiant, by whose rays heaven and [...] are able to see, who measures the underground water for the living beings, who examines their stables, who brings near the distant one.

o 44

[mu]-⸢uṣ-ṣir u₂⸣-<ṣu>-⸢rat ABZU⸣ mu-⸢kin₂⸣ da-⸢ad₂⸣-[me]

o 55

na-din ⸢sur?-qin?-ni?⸣ ana? [...] ⸢mu⸣-qat-te-ru qut-rin-ni

o 66

⸢dUTU ud⸣-da-⸢kam₂⸣ d30 ⸢mu-ši-ti⸣

o 77

⸢ne₂⸣-bi-[i] ⸢nam-ri⸣ ina [ša₂]-ru-⸢ru⸣-šu₂ ⸢i-na⸣-ṭa-lu AN u [...]

o 88

⸢ma-di⸣-[id] ⸢A-MEŠ nag-bi⸣ a-na šik-na-at na-piš-[ti]

o 99

ḫa-a-a-⸢iṭ u₂⸣-ri-⸢ši?⸣-[na] ⸢mu-qar⸣-ri-ib ri-i-⸢qa?⸣

o 1010

d⸢NIN.GIR₂.SU be-el a-la⸣-li mu-šap-<pi>-ik ka-re-e [...]

(o 10) Ningirsu, lord of the work song, the one who piles up grain-heaps [...], reviver of the cripple, raiser of the weak, minder of the people, provider of ..., who gives his ration to the fugitive, the traveller of the road; you are entrusted with the fates of the Igigi, which the lord has decreed, ... [...] ... and Nunura whom he created [...].

o 1111

muTI ⸢a-ki-i⸣ za-⸢qip⸣ en-[ši]

o 1212

ta-ru-u ⸢UN⸣-[MEŠ] e-pi₂-ru x x x

o 1313

⸢ša⸣ a-na ⸢mun-nar-bi⸣ a-lik KASKALni ku-ru-um-⸢ma⸣-[su i]-qiš-⸢šu⸣

o 1414

ši-mat d⸢i₂⸣-[gi₃-gi₃] be-el iq-bu-u qa-⸢tuk-ka paq⸣-[du]

o 1515

PI x x [...] x RU u₃ dNUN.UR₄!(NUN.PAP)RA ip-ti-⸢qu?⸣ [...]

o 1616

⸢e⸣-ma tu-x x x-⸢ka⸣-ma ul i-šag-gi-iš d⸢ER₃⸣.[RA]

(o 16) Wherever you ..., the all-powerful Erra does not slaughter; Ištaran supervises constantly so that he does not bring about a plague.

o 1717

kaš-ka-šu ⸢dKA?⸣.DI i-ḫi-iṭ-ṭa sa-an-tak₂-kam₂-ma ul i-⸢šak⸣-kan ⸢šib⸣-ṭu

o 1818

be-lu₄ ⸢at⸣-[ta]-ma be-el UKKIN nab-ni-ti

(o 18) You are lord, the lord of all creatures, into whose hand the lives of the people are entrusted.

o 1919

ša₂ nap-šat ⸢UN-MEŠ qa-tuk-ka⸣ paq-du

o 2020

⸢te-le⸣-ʾe-⸢e⸣ alan-dim₂-me-⸢e ḪUL⸣ a-na SIG₅ti₃ <<i>>-tu-ru

(o 20) You are able to change an evil physiognomic omen into good fortune;

o 2121

x x x [...] TI SIG TI ur-ru-⸢ku⸣ U₄-MEŠ-šu₂

(o 21) ... [...] ... to lengthen his days;

o 2222

KI.MIN<(te-le-ʾe-e)> sa-ʾa-du ku-uṣ-ṣu-di ma-ḫar ša-ne₂-e

(o 22) Ditto (you are able) to help the cripple, to accept the strange;

o 2323

KI.MIN<(te-le-ʾe-e)> ša-pak ḫup-pi₂ ra-ka-⸢su⸣ bil₂-ta

(o 23) Ditto (you are able) to heap up the scattered, to bind the mixture;

o 2424

KI.MIN<(te-le-ʾe-e)> sa-par!?(ḪI) dša₂-maš UGU qur-⸢da⸣-me ša₂-ka-nu

(o 24) Ditto (you are able) to place the net of Šamaš on the enemy;

o 2525

KI.MIN<(te-le-ʾe-e)> UGU mu-de-e ša₂-ka-nu [šip]-ra aš₂-ṭu

(o 25) Ditto (you are able) to assign difficult work to the initiate;

o 2626

KI.MIN<(te-le-ʾe-e)> la ta-kip sa-an-tak-ki UR? GIŠ? [...] ME? ta-⸢šak⸣-kan ina ma-ḫar um-⸢ma⸣-ni

(o 26) Ditto (you are able) ... [...] ... one who does not impress cuneiform wedges; you place (him) before a scholar;

o 2727

KI.MIN<(te-le-ʾe-e)> ina saḫ-maš-ta te-še-e kub-⸢bu-ub⸣ x x x

(o 27) Ditto (you are able) to burn ... in rebellion (and) confusion;

o 2828

KI.MIN<(te-le-ʾe-e)> ina dGIRA₂ nap-ḫu qar!(AM-)da-me ⸢na⸣-ša₂-a-[ku]

(o 28) Ditto (you are able) to throw the enemies into the kindled fire;

o 2929

KI.MIN<(te-le-ʾe-e)> ina ša₂-ma!?(BAR-)me ma-ku-u₂ ⸢e?⸣-de-e-[...]

(o 29) Ditto (you are able) to neglect ... [...] in the heavens;

o 3030

KI.MIN<(te-le-ʾe-e)> ⸢mun⸣-nab-tu ina KUR la ZUu ša₂-ka-nu ⸢maḫ⸣-[...]

(o 30) Ditto (you are able) to establish the runaway in an unknown land ... [...].

o 3131

ṭar-da na-as-ḫa tur-ru na-an-za-zu qa-tuk-⸢ka⸣ [paq-du]

(o 31) It is entrusted to you to return the fugitive (and) the deportee to (their) position;

o 3232

ša₂ GIM ur-re-e ur-ru-šu ṣap-šiš ub-bu-bu qa-tuk-⸢ka⸣ [paq-du]

(o 32) It is entrusted to you to cleanse like a ṣapšu-garment those who became as dirty as a stable;

o 3333

ša₂ ina up-ša₂-a-ši!?(TI) ub-bu-ru GIM ⸢qe₂-e⸣ KU₃.SI₂₂ nu-um-⸢mu⸣-[ru ...]

(o 33) [...] to make those who are bound with magic shine like a gold thread;

o 3434

ša₂ ina ek-le-ti nu-ru nam-ra šu-ud-gu-⸢lu⸣ [...]

(o 34) [...] to show bright light to those who are in darkness;

o 3535

ša₂ ina un-ṣi gat-ta-šu ⸢ik⸣-kap-⸢pu⸣ nu-uḫ-ša₂ ⸢muḫ⸣-[ḫu-ru ...]

(o 35) [...] to offer abundance to those whose figure is bent from hunger;

o 3636

ša₂ GIM a-ga-al-li su-ru-di ba-ša₂-ma x [...]

(o 36) [...] who ... [...] a sackcloth like a heavily laden donkey;

o 3737

⸢mu-laq⸣-qit še-am mu-ši gišGU.ZA ME TE [...]  ; e-re-bu ⸢i-šak⸣-[...]

(o 37) [...] one who gathers barley together hastily at night, a chair ... [...] ... [...].

Reverse
r 1r 1

[...]

(r 1) [...]

r 22

⸢pu-ḫur kul⸣-la-⸢ti⸣ [...]

(r 2) the totality of it all [...];

r 33

ma-ša₂ ⸢ru-u₂-qa⸣ ša!(IŠ) ru-⸢u₈⸣-[a ...]

(r 3) the forgotten (and) the distant one whom a friend [...];

r 44

ina ⸢gil₂-la-te⸣ MU-<šu₂> <iz>-zak-ru ZU ⸢ŠU?⸣ x [...]

(r 4) they said his name in sin ... [...].

r 55

pa-an MU ⸢UGU⸣ a-me-ri-i šu-uḫ-bu-⸢ṣu⸣ [qa-tuk-ka paq-du]

(r 5) [It is entrusted to you] to make spring pleasing to those who see (it).

r 66

[be]-⸢lu₄⸣ <ša₂> na-⸢zar⸣-bu-ub-šu a-ab-ba <tam>-tum₃-ma ta-⸢ru⸣-[u₂ ...]

(r 6) The lord whose raging is the sea, the ocean, minder [...]

r 77

⸢ma-šit-su la⸣ <pe-tu>-um-ma ⸢ta⸣-ri ta-ru ⸢ta⸣-[...]

(r 7) whose forgotten thing is not revealed and ... [...]

r 88

be-⸢lu₄ ša a-na⸣ muš-<tam>-me!(TAB-)⸢ri⸣-šu a-bi-iš ⸢i⸣-[...]

(r 8) The lord who ... [...] to those who continually praise (him) like a father

r 99

u₃ <a-na> la-si-im EGIR-⸢šu₂⸣ it-ta-nar-⸢ru-u⸣ x [...]

(r 9) and who constantly minds ... [...] to the runner after him.

r 1010

iq-ta-ma UN-ME E₂-ia e-diš ŠEŠ SI [...]

(r 10) The people of my house finished alone ... [...]

r 1111

⸢ša⸣ IRI-ia GIM šal-lat ⸢nak⸣-ri PA GAR x x [...]

(r 11) ... of my city [...] like the enemy's booty.

r 1212

šal-lat UN-⸢ME⸣ IRI-ia ina ⸢la⸣ [...]-ri-u₂-ma LU₂?-šu₂-nu [...]

(r 12) The booty of my city's people without [...] ... their men [...],

r 1313

par-sat kim-⸢tum₃⸣-ma ni-⸢sa-at⸣ a-x [...]

(r 13) the family is cut off and ... [...] is distant [...].

r 1414

ul i-šak-[kin] di-im-tu₂ x ⸢A⸣ ḪI EN RI [...] x x

(r 14) A tear is not put ... [...].

r 1515

is-si-ma [a]-bu ma-ru-šu x x x x AḪ MU x [...] SA

(r 15) A father has called his sons ... [...] ....

r 1616

um-⸢mu ša u₂⸣-rab-bu-u₂-⸢šu⸣ iq-bi-ra [DUMU?]-ša₂

(r 16) The mother who brought him up, buried her [son].

r 1717

ip-te-ma x x x [...] x [...]-ti

(r 17) He opened and ... [...] ... [...] ...

r 1818

be-lu₄ ina na-ra-ru x [...] x x [...] x

(r 18) the lord in aid ... [...] ... [...] ...

r 1919

⸢ul⸣ AŠ₂ x AN x x x [...] PAD

(r 19) not ... [...] ...

r 2020

[...]-ar-⸢ši⸣-na x x x x [...] x LU

(r 20) [...] ... [...] ...

r 2121

ša sa-⸢ar⸣-ri ⸢ke⸣-e-ni x x x [...] NA

(r 21) of the criminal (and) the honest one ... [...] ....

r 2222

[be]-lu₄ a-šar x [...] x x [...] x

(r 22) Lord, where ... [...] ... [...] ...

r 2323

x x x x [...] x x x [...] x

(r 23) ... [...] ... [...] ...

r 2424

⸢ša₂ ina⸣ KU A x x [...] A

(r 24) who in ... [...] ...

r 2525

x x ŠA ⸢AS? ḪI?⸣ x [...] x x x [...]

(r 25) ... [...] ... [...]

r 2626

x ⸢ŠA⸣ AS KU [...]-ḫi-iš [...]

(r 26) ... [...] ... [...]

r 2727

lu KI.⸢MIN⸣ sa-sa-x [...] x x x [...]

(r 27) or alternatively ... [...] ... [...]

r 2828

it-⸢ti⸣ x KU x x [...] x [...]

(r 28) with ... [...] ... [...]

r 2929

x x x [...] LUGAL x x x x x [...]

(r 29) ... [...] king ... [...].

(colophon)
r 3030

[...] x x [...] x [...] x x [...]

(r 30) [...] ... [...] ... [...] ... [...]

r 3131

[...] ⸢li-gi-mu-u₂⸣ [...] x x [...]

(r 31) [...] descendant of [...] ... [...], son of ..., scribe of Huzirina [...], son of Šulmu-bel-lašme, scribe.

r 3232

⸢PEŠ⸣ mDU₃-x-[...] ⸢ṭup⸣-šar₃ ⸢iri⸣ḫu-zir-⸢na⸣ [...]

r 3333

⸢PEŠ?⸣ mSILIMmu—⸢EN?⸣—la-aš₂-me lu₂A.BA

r 3434

[ša] ⸢i⸣-tab-⸢bal dša₃-maš₂ IGI-MIN-šu₂ lit⸣-bal [...]

(r 34) [Whoever] takes away (this tablet), may Šamaš take away his eyes. [...]

r 3535

x x x x x x [...] x x x x x x [...] x [...]

(r 35) ... [...] ... [...] ... [...]

r 3636

[...] x x AD? [...] x MAN KUR [aš]-⸢šur?⸣

(r 36) [...] ... [...] ... king of Assyria.
